pr.title: Match 22 functions across stage, fighter, menu, match-end, and synth TUs
pr.author: davidfeira

summary: PR #2462 matched a broad batch of functions across stage, fighter, menu, match-end/game-mode, vi, and sysdolphin synth translation units, alongside typed header refinements and one file-local layout struct needed by the new matches. The PR body reports a SHA1-matching ninja build, per-function objdiff-cli 100.0% matches for the claimed 22 functions, and an adjacent-regression fix involving removal of a cherry-picked dont_inline pragma.

key_files.role: Matched throw-release effect dispatcher fn_800D84D4.
Matched ftKb_SpecialN_800F1DAC and updated caller to pass gobj.
Replaced UNK prototype with void ftKb_SpecialN_800F1DAC(HSD_GObj*).
Matched Kirby Special-Lw collision handlers and added file-local layout struct for ftKb_Init_803CB490 data access.
Matched Yoshi-egg shrink animation tick fn_8010AA64.
Matched multiple match-end reducers/accumulators/tiebreaker helpers and fn_80169F50 in diff excerpt.
Typed prototypes for fn_80161004, fn_80162068, and fn_80161C90.
Matched tournament-event slot initializer and end-of-round arbiter.
Matched per-stage anime applier grAnime_801C8138.
Matched Home-Run Contest stage initializer and score readout builder.
Typed prototype for grHomeRun_8021EC58.
Matched Ice Mountain animation tracker, scroll state machine, and per-frame scroll driver.
Updated grIceMt_801FA364 signature to bool return and typed arguments.
Matched warning-dialog text setter and input handler.
Typed prototype for mnDataDel_8024EBC8.
Matched match-rule snapshot collector.
Typed prototype for mn_80230D18.
Matched Mushroom-Kingdom-Route demo-character setup un_8031F294.
Typed prototype for un_8031F294.
Matched SFX-bank stream-prefetch finalizer and prefetched-stream header callback.
Header/static definition refinement associated with synth matches.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Summary mentions inline struct/header refinements; changed file metadata

what_changed: Replaced placeholder function markers with matched C implementations across 13 translation units: gricemt, grhomerun, granime, ftCo_Attack100, ftKb_Init, ftKb_SpecialN, ftKb_SpecialNYs, mndatadel, mnmainrule, gm_1601, gm_1BA8, vi1101, and synth.
Refined headers by replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or speculative signatures with typed prototypes in gricemt.h, mnmainrule.h, vi1101.h, ftKb_Init.h, gm_1601.h, grhomerun.h, and mndatadel.h.
Added a file-local struct ftKb_Init_803CB490_layout with padding to offset 0x74 and a Vec3 field so Kirby Special-Lw code could dereference a known data layout instead of raw untyped access.

Used explicit gameplay-state logic in matched bodies: collision edge detection from env_flags/prev_env_flags, match-end standings loops, tournament event flag unpacking, menu input masks, JObj animation/scale operations, and AX/synth voice setup.

Verification reported in the PR body: ninja produced build-linux/GALE01/main.dol OK with SHA1 match; per-function objdiff-cli reported 100.0% for each of the 22 claimed functions; an adjacent fuzzy regression caused by a dont_inline pragma was corrected by removing the pragma.
smart_moves: Updated owning headers when matched bodies proved real signatures, rather than hiding typed calls behind source-local declarations.
Kept a narrow file-local data layout for ftKb_Init_803CB490 access, making the recovered Vec3 offset explicit without claiming a broader global type than the PR evidence proves.

Checked adjacent regression impact from a pragma/partial-match side effect and removed the dont_inline pragma when it degraded fn_800DAD18, preserving the upstream partial for ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys instead of landing a noisy local win.
Grouped broad function matches with a detailed PR body that records what each recovered function does and what verification was run.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matched body proves a function signature, update the owning header prototypes at the same time so call sites can use typed arguments and return values.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: ftKb_Init.h and gm_1601.h prototype changes

decomp_lessons.lesson: A temporary, file-local layout struct can be a reviewable bridge for known data offsets when the match needs typed field access but the full global type is not recovered.
Pragma or inlining tactics should be checked against adjacent functions; if a tactic regresses a neighbor, remove it even if it was bundled with another partial match.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Verification paragraph about fn_800DAD18 regression and dont_inline pragma removal

decomp_lessons.lesson: For broad multi-TU match batches, record per-function objdiff evidence and a whole-build SHA1 result because the blast radius spans unrelated systems.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: 21 changed files across multiple subsystems

naming_conventions.convention: Preserve address-style names such as fn_800D84D4, fn_8010AA64, fn_80161004, and un_8031F294 where semantic names are not proven by the PR evidence.
Use short role locals for common object pointers such as fp, gobj, jobj, coll, src, and match_end when the role is clear.
For temporary recovered data layouts tied to a known symbol, a symbol-derived name with _layout suffix was used: ftKb_Init_803CB490_layout.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was used in several matched functions to preserve stack shape, including fn_800D84D4, ftKb_SpecialN_800F1DAC, ftKb_SpecialLw_Coll, ftKb_SpecialLwEnd_Coll, and gm_801BD46C.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The PR body reports per-function 100.0% objdiff matches, but the loaded diff is truncated and does not independently show every claimed use.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Typed header cleanup accompanied matched implementation work, replacing UNK prototypes with signatures required by call sites and bodies.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: Header refinements are described by the PR author and partially visible in the diff excerpt.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A file-local padded struct exposed a Vec3 at offset 0x74 for ftKb_Init_803CB490, avoiding repeated raw offset spelling in the two Kirby Special-Lw handlers.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: The evidence supports this as a local bridge, not a fully recovered global data type.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Adjacent-function regression checking was used to reject a dont_inline pragma that reduced fn_800DAD18 from 100% to 90.31%.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caveat: No separate review comment is loaded; this is from the PR body verification note.

review_feedback.feedback: No human review comments or reviews are present in the loaded PR slice.

decomp-dev[bot] issue comment reported matched code improvement to 66.21% (+0.25%, +9608 bytes) and matched data to 38.70% (+0.00%, +8 bytes); the loaded bot report is truncated but lists new matches.
Author reported an initial CI/fuzzy regression from a dont_inline pragma, removed the pragma, restored fn_800DAD18 to 100%, and left ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys at upstream's 49.25% partial.

notes: The PR body is detailed and the changed-file list is complete, but the raw diff and text corpus are truncated in the loaded slice.

The PR body claims 22 matched functions, while the automated bot excerpt says 25 new matches; because the bot report is truncated, this record preserves the PR title/body claim and notes the discrepancy only as evidence quality context.
No human review comments are loaded, so no reviewer intent or acceptance rationale is inferred.
Verification claims are author-provided in the PR body; no independent build logs or full objdiff outputs are loaded beyond the bot report excerpt.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Header signatures should follow proven matched bodies
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2462 updated multiple owning headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or speculative signatures to typed prototypes when the matched bodies and call sites proved the arguments and return types.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Reject pragma tactics that regress adjacent functions
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2462 reports that a dont_inline pragma cherry-picked with a partial match regressed fn_800DAD18 from 100% to 90.31%; the pragma was removed and the adjacent function restored, while the partial match reverted to upstream's score.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use narrow local layout structs as temporary typed-offset bridges
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2462 introduced a file-local ftKb_Init_803CB490_layout with padding to 0x74 and a Vec3 field so two Kirby Special-Lw handlers could access the same recovered constant Vec3 layout.

source_update_candidates.title: Use PR #2462 as an example for pragma regression checks
source_update_candidates.text: Potential example for the avoid-pragmas/register-asm or verification ledger standard: a dont_inline pragma bundled with partial decomp work caused an adjacent function regression, so the tactic was removed and the adjacent 100% match restored.
source_update_candidates.reason: The existing standards already cover this principle; the PR provides a concrete historical example for curator consideration.

source_update_candidates.title: Use PR #2462 as an example for typed headers after body recovery
source_update_candidates.text: Potential example for truthful-headers-and-includes: this PR matched functions and updated owning headers such as ftKb_Init.h and gm_1601.h from unknown or stale prototypes to signatures used by the recovered bodies.
source_update_candidates.reason: The standards already say to update owning headers; this PR is a candidate illustrative source, not a new accepted rule.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR's temporary ftKb_Init_803CB490_layout as a complete global type; the evidence only supports a file-local bridge with a Vec3 at offset 0x74.
Do not infer human reviewer approval details; the loaded slice has no human reviews or review comments.
Do not treat the bot's truncated '25 new matches' line as replacing the PR's '22 matched functions' claim without the full automated report.
